title: PR-1 continuous Simulation Worker session scheduler
summary: Adapt sim-worker to host the PR-1 GameSessionRuntime with continuous fixed-30-TPS scheduling, pause/speed semantics, snapshot backpressure and Worker/headless parity using existing message families.
acceptance: Simulation Worker can initialize and host the PR-1 GameSessionRuntime from WM-0163 through existing InitSession/LoadSession/PlayerCommandBatch/SetSpeed/Pause/RequestUiDetail/RequestSave/Shutdown families without public schema drift
Worker owns normal product scheduling: fixed 30 TPS at speed 1, 60 at speed 2, 90 at speed 3, and 0 when paused or speed 0; sim-core receives only integer tick advancement
Pause preserves requested speed while stopping automatic ticks, SetSpeed affects future scheduling only, and command results remain reliable around pause/speed changes
RenderSnapshot/UiDelta publication is latest-wins under backpressure, while CommandResult, SaveReady, FatalSimulationError and structured alerts are not dropped
WM-0160 explicit advance/drain helpers remain available for tests/tools but are not used as the default gameplay clock
Worker and Node headless produce matching checkpoint world/read-model hashes for the same seed, scenario definition and fixed command stream
RequestSave/LoadSession for GameSession either use an explicitly versioned focused PR-1 snapshot or fail closed with structured unsupported reasons; no public save compatibility promise is added
Browser Worker smoke proves real Chromium Worker scheduling emits advancing PR-1 GameSession projections without UI dispatching advance helper commands as normal time
No apps/web product UI, renderer-pixi, ui-react, Electron preload, public protocol/schema/save, new runtime dependency or release action is changed
The report records scheduler policy, backpressure behavior, reliable-message evidence, parity hashes and any residual PR-1 blockers

history.details: Scoped as the Worker host/scheduler task after WM-0163; remains proposed and must not introduce protocol/schema/save drift without a new gate.

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. RequestSave/LoadSession behavior for GameSession must be versioned focused evidence or fail-closed unsupported behavior.
forbiddenPaths: new public Worker message family or incompatible protocol/schema payload without a new reviewed ADR
apps/** product UI implementation, renderer-pixi/ui-react changes or Electron preload changes
sim-core runtime ownership changes beyond consuming WM-0163 public root exports
using WM-0160 drain/advance helpers as the default gameplay clock
hidden global Worker/session singleton, timer authority inside sim-core, dropped reliable messages or UI-owned simulation state
public save compatibility, migration, release, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback surfaces
